title: Pokhara 7-Day Adventure Tour
metaTitle: Pokhara 7-Day Adventure Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Pokhara, Nepal: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Sarangkot sunrise viewpoint and World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa). Prices from $1464 per person (estimate).
highlights: Paragliding from Sarangkot over Phewa Lake
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Pokhara, Nepal — Subtropical climate at a lower elevation (~820m) than Kathmandu, so noticeably warmer and more humid: hot, wet summers (highs 29-30°C with heavy monsoon rain June-September, as Pokhara is one of Nepal's wettest cities), and mild, drier winters (highs 20-22°C). October-November and March-April for the clearest views of the Annapurna range and mild highs of 24-28°C; the June-August monsoon frequently obscures mountain views with cloud. This itinerary blends Sarangkot sunrise viewpoint, World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa), Lakeside (Phewa Lake promenade) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Sarangkot sunrise viewpoint, World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa), Lakeside (Phewa Lake promenade)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Pokhara
itinerary.description: Land at Pokhara International Airport (PKR) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Sarangkot sunrise viewpoint — Hilltop viewpoint over the Annapurna and Machapuchare ranges. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Pokhara: World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa) & Davis Falls (Patale Chhango)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa) — Hilltop Buddhist stupa reached by boat and hike or road. In the afternoon, continue to Davis Falls (Patale Chhango) — Dramatic waterfall that disappears into an underground tunnel. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Pokhara: Gupteshwor Mahadev Cave & International Mountain Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Gupteshwor Mahadev Cave — Limestone cave with a Shiva shrine, connected to Davis Falls. In the afternoon, continue to International Mountain Museum — Exhibits on Himalayan mountaineering history and culture. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Sarangkot
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Sarangkot, a popular side trip from Pokhara. Return to Pokhara in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Begnas Lake, quieter than Phewa — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Pokhara: Phewa Lake boating & Paragliding from Sarangkot
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Phewa Lake boating — Rowing or paddle boat across Pokhara's central lake. In the afternoon, continue to Paragliding from Sarangkot — Tandem paragliding over Phewa Lake with Annapurna views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Lakeside (Phewa Lake promenade) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to PKR for your departure flight.

travelTips: Head to Sarangkot before dawn for the best chance of a clear, cloud-free Annapurna sunrise.
Book paragliding for the morning — winds tend to pick up and visibility can drop in the afternoon.
Pokhara is the standard starting point for the Annapurna Circuit and Poon Hill treks — arrange permits (TIMS card, ACAP) in town.
The 25-minute flight from Kathmandu saves a full day versus the winding mountain road.
Begnas Lake offers a quieter, less touristy alternative to Phewa Lake for a half-day escape.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Layered trekking-friendly clothing given the range of altitudes nearby
Rain gear for the heavy June-September monsoon
Comfortable trekking shoes even for short day hikes to Sarangkot
Sunglasses and sun protection for high-altitude viewpoints
thingsToDo: Paragliding from Sarangkot over Phewa Lake
Trekking toward Annapurna Base Camp or Poon Hill
Zip-lining, one of the world's longest and steepest
White-water rafting on the Seti or Trishuli rivers
Phewa Lake boat ride to Tal Barahi Temple
Davis Falls and Gupteshwor Cave visit
thingsToAvoid: Booking cut-rate trekking guides without proper licensing or insurance for high-altitude routes.
Paragliding with unlicensed operators — verify APPI/PPA certification.
Expecting mountain views during the monsoon (Jun-Sep) — cloud cover is the norm, not the exception.
Swimming in Phewa Lake far from designated areas — depths and currents vary.
Underestimating altitude effects if continuing straight onto a high trek without acclimatization.
nightlife: Sunset drinks at rooftop restaurants overlooking Phewa Lake
instagramSpots: Tal Barahi Temple on the lake
familyActivities: Phewa Lake boat ride to Tal Barahi Temple
Davis Falls and Gupteshwor Cave visit
adventureActivities: Paragliding from Sarangkot over Phewa Lake
Trekking toward Annapurna Base Camp or Poon Hill
Zip-lining, one of the world's longest and steepest
White-water rafting on the Seti or Trishuli rivers
luxuryExperiences: Lakeside luxury resort stay with Annapurna views
Private helicopter mountain flight over the Annapurna range
Private sunrise tour to Sarangkot with breakfast
Fine dining at a boutique lakeside hotel
honeymoonExperiences: Private boat ride on Phewa Lake at sunset
Candlelight dinner lakeside at Boomerang Restaurant
budgetExperiences: Free lakeside promenade walk at Phewa Lake
Shared taxi/bus trip to Sarangkot for sunrise
Rowboat rental split among a small group
localEtiquette: Dress modestly and remove shoes at temples such as Bindhyabasini and the World Peace Pagoda.
Respect trekking-trail etiquette — yield the uphill side to pack animals (mules/dzos) and greet fellow trekkers with "Namaste".
Support locally-run teahouses and guesthouses on trekking routes rather than only large chains.
Ask permission before photographing porters or guides at work.
localTransportGuide: Lakeside is compact and walkable, with taxis and rented scooters covering trips to Sarangkot or the airport; trekking trailheads (for Annapurna routes) are reached by local jeep or taxi from town. Tourist buses connect Pokhara to Kathmandu (roughly 6-7 hours by road, or a short 25-minute flight).
bestTimeToVisit: October-November and March-April for the clearest views of the Annapurna range and mild highs of 24-28°C; the June-August monsoon frequently obscures mountain views with cloud.
weather: Subtropical climate at a lower elevation (~820m) than Kathmandu, so noticeably warmer and more humid: hot, wet summers (highs 29-30°C with heavy monsoon rain June-September, as Pokhara is one of Nepal's wettest cities), and mild, drier winters (highs 20-22°C).
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals can get a Nepal visa on arrival (paid in cash or card) at major entry points; Indian citizens need no visa at all to enter Nepal, only a valid photo ID, thanks to the open-border treaty between the two countries.
averageFlightTime: ~9.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Pokhara?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals can get a Nepal visa on arrival (paid in cash or card) at major entry points; Indian citizens need no visa at all to enter Nepal, only a valid photo ID, thanks to the open-border treaty between the two countries.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Pokhara?
faqs.answer: October-November and March-April for the clearest views of the Annapurna range and mild highs of 24-28°C; the June-August monsoon frequently obscures mountain views with cloud. Subtropical climate at a lower elevation (~820m) than Kathmandu, so noticeably warmer and more humid: hot, wet summers (highs 29-30°C with heavy monsoon rain June-September, as Pokhara is one of Nepal's wettest cities), and mild, drier winters (highs 20-22°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Pokhara safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Pokhara is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Pokhara?
faqs.answer: The local currency is NPR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Pokhara is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunrise together at Sarangkot experience.

reviews.author: Arjun Müller
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Old Pokhara Bazaar in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ethan Ivanov
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Davis Falls (Patale Chhango) and Sarangkot sunrise viewpoint. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Wang
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Old Pokhara Bazaar this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: James Costa
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Pokhara was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Yuki Rossi
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Sarangkot sunrise viewpoint alone was worth the trip, but combined with Davis Falls (Patale Chhango) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Grace Müller
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Phewa Lake shoreline and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: David Larsen
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Pokhara is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Tal Barahi Temple exterior view. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Omar Müller
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Bindhyabasini Temple and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
